correct_explanation: the FIR-style state and tap arrays create extreme register pressure, forcing spills to local memory
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: register-spill
REASONING: The kernel uses 255 registers per thread with only 15.19% achieved occupancy, which strongly indicates extreme register pressure from the large per-thread arrays and aggressive unrolling. Despite low arithmetic intensity relative to the ridge point, DRAM utilization is only 0.67% and memory accesses are efficient with 100% load efficiency and 100% L2 hit rate, so the dominant issue is spilled/local-memory-backed register pressure causing the very high 89.75% long scoreboard stalls.
parsed_reasoning: The kernel uses 255 registers per thread with only 15.19% achieved occupancy, which strongly indicates extreme register pressure from the large per-thread arrays and aggressive unrolling. Despite low arithmetic intensity relative to the ridge point, DRAM utilization is only 0.67% and memory accesses are efficient with 100% load efficiency and 100% L2 hit rate, so the dominant issue is spilled/local-memory-backed register pressure causing the very high 89.75% long scoreboard stalls.
